[[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]] Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:三上氏]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]] Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the [[:en:Yamana clan|Yamana clan]] of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:三上氏]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}} Mikami clan
{{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} Mikami retainers ([[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]])
[[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]] Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the [[:en:Yamana clan|Yamana clan]] of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:三上氏]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] Mikami clan of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] lineage |家祖= {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |種別= {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenson tribe|ja|天孫族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson tribe|1=Tenson-zoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939350}}) |出身地= [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |根拠地= [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]

{{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} Mikami retainers ([[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]])
[[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]] Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the [[:en:Yamana clan|Yamana clan]] of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:三上氏]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:三上氏]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}}

[[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]] Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the [[:en:Yamana clan|Yamana clan]] of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:三上氏]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}}

This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the [[:en:Yamana clan|Yamana clan]] of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:三上氏]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}}

[[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]] Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the [[:en:Yamana clan|Yamana clan]] of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:三上氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

{{ill|Tōzawa Tadazane|ja|戸沢正諶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzawa Tadazane|1=Tozawa Masanobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514159}} |支流= {{ill|Hiroshima Domain|ja|藝藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Mikami retainers
[[:en:Rikuo|Mutsu Province]] Mikami clan
[[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] Mikami retainers
{{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1208689}} Mikami clan
{{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} Mikami retainers ([[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]])
[[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]] Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]][[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi Province]]{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of [[:en:Mikami Shrine|Mikami Shrine]], historically tied to [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]]. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]], the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the [[:en:Yamana clan|Yamana clan]] of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三上氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}, the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|WD=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan}} of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三上氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|WD=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain}} Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Yasu District, Shiga|en|Yasu District, Shiga|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District, Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of {{ill|Mikami Shrine|en|Mikami Shrine|WD=Q11488762|lt=Mikami Shrine}}, historically tied to {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}, the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|WD=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan}} of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三上氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|Bakufu|ja|幕臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|1=bakushin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481849}} Mikami retainers ({{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto}})
{{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|WD=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain}} Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Yasu District, Shiga|en|Yasu District, Shiga|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District, Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of {{ill|Mikami Shrine|en|Mikami Shrine|WD=Q11488762|lt=Mikami Shrine}}, historically tied to {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}, the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|WD=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan}} of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三上氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}, the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|WD=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan|1=Yamana clan}} of Tajima Province. Their presence is documented from 1456 (Kōshō 2).{{Sfn|Takahashi|1986|p=}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōta|editor-first=Ryo|editor-link=太田亮|title=Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten|volume=3|publisher=Kokoku Shinsha|year=1936}} * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=Ōtani|editor-first=Jisaku|editor-link=大谷治作|title=Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|publisher=Ōtani Jisaku|year=1899}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{translated page|ja|三上氏|version=103285870|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三上氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11354125}} [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Mikami Clan]] [[Category:Servitors of the Muromachi Shogunate]] [[Category:Uda Genji Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|WD=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain|1=Kaga Domain}} Mikami retainers }} '''Mikami clan''' (三上氏) was a historical {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine |image = |氏姓 = Mikami {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} ({{ill|Hōri|ja|祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōri|qq=}}) |始祖 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} |出自 = |氏祖 = Takihiko no Mikoto |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Yasu District, Shiga|en|Yasu District, Shiga|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District, Ōmi Province|1=Yasu District, Shiga}}{{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1936|p=3745}} |人物 = Mikami Kaeo |後裔 = }} The Mikami clan of Mikami Shrine was a group based in {{ill|Mikami|ja|三上郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yasu District, Ōmi Province. This clan served as the priestly lineage of {{ill|Mikami Shrine|en|Mikami Shrine|WD=Q11488762|lt=Mikami Shrine|1=Mikami Shrine}}, historically tied to {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami|1=Mount Mikami}}. Mikami Shrine claims the clan descends from {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}'s third son, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, and his descendant {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}}, who is said to have descended to Mount Mikami. According to {{ill|Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja|御上神社沿革考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Shrine Historical Examination|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mount Mikami is believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.{{Sfn|Ōtani|1899|p=3}} == "Mikami" in ancient records == The term "Mikami" appears in several ancient texts. While some references pertain to people or places like Mikami District in {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}, the connection to Ōmi Province is sparse. The most notable is a mention of "Mikami Shrine" in {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in 865 (Jōgan 7) and 875 (Jōgan 17).{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992a|p=}}{{Sfn|Ujitani|1992b|p=}}{{Sfn|Morita|2006a|p=}} == Mikami clan of Inaba Province == The Inaba Mikami clan, based in {{ill|Iwai District|ja|岩井庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is believed to have been deputies sent by the {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|WD=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan|1=Yamana clan}} of Tajima Province.
